task_completion.description: Verify the OpenICE Supervisor device list shows a simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open OpenICE Supervisor GUI window with a device list that includes a 'Multiparameter Monitor' or similar multi-parameter vital signs simulator.

task_completion.description: Verify the OpenICE Supervisor device list shows a simulated NIBP device adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open OpenICE Supervisor GUI window with a device list that includes an 'NIBP' (Non-Invasive Blood Pressure) simulator.

task_completion.description: Verify the Vital Signs clinical application is launched and displays data from the simulated devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Vital Signs application window showing active clinical data (e.g., waveforms, numeric values for heart rate, blood pressure) corresponding to the simulated devices.

task_completion.description: Verify the two required screenshot files are saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'conformance_evidence_vitals.png' and 'conformance_evidence_devices.png' icons on the Desktop, or visible in a file manager/terminal listing of /home/ga/Desktop/.

task_completion.description: Verify the dec_conformance_report.txt file is created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'dec_conformance_report.txt' on the Desktop or open in a text editor.

task_completion.description: Verify the conformance report contains the required sections and mentions DDS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of the text report (if visible in an editor or terminal) and check for the headings: TEST IDENTIFICATION, DEVICE UNDER TEST (DUT) INVENTORY, CLINICAL APPLICATION (DOC) CONFIGURATION, DATA FLOW VERIFICATION RESULTS, OVERALL CONFORMANCE ASSESSMENT, and the mention of 'DDS'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenICE Supervisor GUI to create the simulated devices.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the OpenICE Supervisor interface is a real application window and not a mocked text file or terminal output masquerading as the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify the Vital Signs application is a genuine GUI window displaying clinical data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Vital Signs app is an actual graphical application showing simulated medical data, rather than a static image opened in an image viewer before the task required it.

integrity.description: Verify the conformance report is formatted as a formal report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the text in dec_conformance_report.txt is structured like a formal test report with the required sections filled out, rather than just a raw copy-paste of the prompt's instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: DOR stands for Device Observation Reporter and DOC stands for Device Observation Consumer. NIBP stands for Non-Invasive Blood Pressure. DDS (Data Distribution Service) is the middleware standard used for publish-subscribe device communication in OpenICE. The expected files are exactly: /home/ga/Desktop/dec_conformance_report.txt, /home/ga/Desktop/conformance_evidence_vitals.png, and /home/ga/Desktop/conformance_evidence_devices.png. The report must contain the exact sections: TEST IDENTIFICATION, DEVICE UNDER TEST (DUT) INVENTORY, CLINICAL APPLICATION (DOC) CONFIGURATION, DATA FLOW VERIFICATION RESULTS, and OVERALL CONFORMANCE ASSESSMENT.
